ip_outcome_note: Confirmed. Nosepass is a Game Freak-internal species (National Pokedex no. 299, Generation III, Hoenn). Rock-type Compass Pokemon with Easter Island Moai-inspired design. No external IP concern.

trivia_bullets.text: Nosepass is National Pokedex no. 299, the Compass Pokemon, a Rock-type introduced in Generation III (Hoenn). It evolves into Probopass when leveled up in a magnetic field location. [Pokemondb.net: Nosepass, via WebSearch; Bulbapedia: Nosepass, via WebSearch]

Flavor text (this print): "It hunts without twitching a muscle by pulling in its prey with powerful magnetism. But sometimes it pulls natural enemies in close." [Frontmatter flavor_text field, PokemonTCG.io]

Nosepass's design is based on two sources: the Moai statues of Easter Island (block-head silhouette, stoic forward-facing posture) and a compass (the large red nose always points north — functioning as a magnetic navigation device in lore). [Bulbapedia: Nosepass, via WebSearch snippet; Pokemondb.net: Nosepass, via WebSearch]

Probopass, Nosepass's evolution, extends the compass metaphor: its three Mini-Noses point East, South, and West, completing a full compass rose with the main nose pointing North. Probopass also gains a Steel type, making the pair Rock and Rock/Steel — an unusual split between unevolved and evolved type compositions. [Pokemondb.net: Probopass, via WebSearch; Bulbapedia: Probopass, via WebSearch]

related_cards.name: Probopass
related_cards.note: Nosepass's evolution; Rock/Steel type with full compass-rose Mini-Nose design
